name: Turmeric
preparation: Mix 1 tsp turmeric powder in warm milk or water. Take once or twice daily.
safety: Generally safe. Avoid large doses during pregnancy. May interact with blood thinners.

name: Ginger
preparation: Brew 1-2 slices fresh ginger in hot water for 10 min. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
safety: Generally safe. May cause heartburn in high doses. Avoid before surgery.

name: Ashwagandha
preparation: Take 300-500mg extract daily with warm milk or water.
safety: Avoid during pregnancy. May interact with thyroid medications.

name: Holy Basil
preparation: Brew 5-6 fresh leaves or 1 tsp dried in hot water. Drink 2x daily.

name: Echinacea
preparation: Take 300mg capsule 3x daily at onset of cold symptoms for up to 10 days.
safety: Avoid with autoimmune conditions. Not for long-term use.

name: Valerian Root
preparation: Take 300-600mg extract 30-60 minutes before bed.
safety: May cause morning grogginess. Avoid with sedatives or alcohol.

name: Chamomile
preparation: Steep 1 tbsp dried flowers in hot water for 5 min. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
safety: May cause allergic reaction in those allergic to ragweed.

name: Lavender
preparation: Use as essential oil in diffuser or brew 1 tsp dried flowers as tea before bed.
safety: Avoid undiluted essential oil on skin. Generally safe as tea.

name: Peppermint
preparation: Steep 1 tbsp leaves in hot water 5 min. Apply diluted oil to temples for headache.
safety: Avoid with acid reflux. Do not apply near infants' faces.

name: Milk Thistle
preparation: Take 140mg silymarin extract 3x daily with meals.
safety: Generally safe. May cause mild laxative effect.

name: St. John's Wort
preparation: Take 300mg standardized extract 3x daily.
safety: Interacts with many medications. Do not use with antidepressants.

name: Oregano
preparation: Use as essential oil diluted in carrier oil. Brew as tea with 1 tsp dried herb.
safety: Strong — dilute essential oil. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Rosemary
preparation: Brew as tea or apply diluted oil to scalp. Use in cooking daily.
safety: Avoid large medicinal doses in pregnancy.

name: Thyme
preparation: Brew 1 tsp dried thyme in hot water 10 min. Drink 3x daily for cough.
safety: Generally safe. Avoid high doses in pregnancy.

name: Sage
preparation: Brew 1 tsp dried sage in hot water. Gargle for sore throat or drink for memory.
safety: Avoid in epilepsy. Limit in pregnancy.

name: Lemon Balm
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried herb in hot water 10 min. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
safety: Generally safe. May interact with thyroid medications.

name: Passionflower
preparation: Take 250mg extract or brew as tea 30-60 min before bed.
safety: May interact with sedatives. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Elderberry
preparation: Take 1 tbsp elderberry syrup 4x daily during illness or 1x daily preventively.
safety: Do not eat raw berries. Safe when cooked.

name: Calendula
preparation: Apply calendula cream topically or brew as tea for internal use.
safety: May cause allergic reaction in those allergic to daisies.

name: Nettle
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried nettle in hot water. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
safety: May interact with blood thinners and diuretics.

name: Red Clover
preparation: Take 40-160mg isoflavone extract daily.

name: Black Cohosh
preparation: Take 20-40mg extract twice daily.
safety: Avoid in liver disease or pregnancy. Limit use to 6 months.

name: Feverfew
preparation: Take 50-100mg daily for migraine prevention. Chew fresh leaves.
safety: May cause mouth ulcers with fresh leaves. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Cat's Claw
preparation: Take 250-350mg extract 2x daily.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy and with immunosuppressants.

name: Devil's Claw
preparation: Take 600-800mg extract 2-3x daily.
safety: Avoid in stomach ulcers, pregnancy. May interact with blood thinners.

name: Boswellia
preparation: Take 300-400mg standardized extract 3x daily.
safety: Generally safe. May cause GI discomfort.

name: Andrographis
preparation: Take 400mg extract 3x daily at onset of illness.
safety: Avoid in autoimmune conditions and pregnancy.

name: Astragalus
preparation: Take 500mg extract 2x daily or brew root as tea.

name: Dong Quai
preparation: Take 3-4g dried root as decoction or 1g extract daily.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy. May increase bleeding risk.

name: Schisandra
preparation: Take 500mg-2g extract daily.
safety: Generally safe. May interact with some medications.

name: Rhodiola
preparation: Take 200-400mg standardized extract before meals.
safety: Avoid in bipolar disorder. May cause agitation.

name: Eleuthero
preparation: Take 300-600mg extract daily for up to 3 months.
safety: Generally safe. Avoid with high blood pressure.

name: Licorice Root
preparation: Take 1-3g dried root as tea or 200mg DGL extract before meals.
safety: Avoid in high blood pressure. Long-term use may cause electrolyte imbalance.

name: Dandelion
preparation: Brew root as tea or add fresh leaves to salads. Take 500mg extract 2x daily.
safety: May interact with diuretics and lithium.

name: Burdock Root
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried root as tea 2x daily or take 500mg extract.

name: Yellow Dock
preparation: Brew 1 tsp root in hot water 20 min. Take 250mg extract 2x daily.
safety: Avoid in kidney stones. May cause laxative effect.

name: Hawthorn
preparation: Take 160-900mg extract daily. Brew berries as tea.
safety: May interact with heart medications. Consult doctor.

name: Motherwort
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried herb as tea or take 150mg extract 3x daily.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy and with heart medications.

name: Skullcap
preparation: Brew as tea or take 350mg extract 3x daily.

name: Wood Betony
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried herb in hot water 10 min.

name: Marshmallow Root
preparation: Cold-infuse in water overnight or brew as tea. Take 6g dried root daily.
safety: May delay absorption of other medications.

name: Slippery Elm
preparation: Mix 1 tsp powder in water to form gruel. Take before meals.

name: Pau d'Arco
preparation: Brew 1 tbsp bark in 1 liter water 20 min. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
safety: Avoid in blood clotting disorders and pregnancy.

name: Uva Ursi
preparation: Take 400-840mg extract 3x daily for up to 5 days.
safety: Not for long-term use. Avoid in pregnancy and kidney disease.

name: Corn Silk
preparation: Brew 2 tsp dried silk in hot water 10 min. Drink 3x daily.
safety: Generally safe. May interact with diuretics.

name: Cleavers
preparation: Brew fresh plant as tea or take as tincture 2-4ml 3x daily.

name: Chickweed
preparation: Apply as poultice or ointment to skin. Brew as tea for internal use.

name: Plantain
preparation: Apply crushed leaves to wounds. Brew as tea for respiratory issues.

name: Yarrow
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried herb as tea. Apply to wounds as poultice.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy. May cause skin sensitivity.

name: Goldenrod
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried herb as tea 2-3x daily.
safety: May interact with diuretics and anti-diabetes drugs.

name: Meadowsweet
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp as tea 3x daily after meals.

name: Cinnamon
preparation: Add 1/2-1 tsp to food or brew as tea daily.
safety: High doses may cause liver damage. Use Ceylon cinnamon preferably.

name: Cloves
preparation: Apply clove oil to toothache. Use in cooking or brew as tea.
safety: Avoid undiluted clove oil on skin. Do not ingest clove oil in large amounts.

name: Black Pepper
preparation: Add to food daily. Combine with turmeric for enhanced absorption.

name: Cardamom
preparation: Chew pods or brew crushed seeds in hot water as tea.

name: Fenugreek
preparation: Take 5-30g seeds daily or 500mg extract 2x daily.
safety: May lower blood sugar significantly. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Cumin
preparation: Add 1 tsp to meals or brew as tea.

name: Coriander
preparation: Add seeds or leaves to food. Brew seeds as tea.

name: Fennel
preparation: Brew 1 tsp crushed seeds in hot water. Chew seeds after meals.
safety: Avoid high doses in pregnancy and with estrogen-sensitive conditions.

name: Mustard
preparation: Apply mustard paste to chest for congestion. Use in cooking.
safety: May irritate skin. Avoid in kidney inflammation.

name: Allspice
preparation: Use in cooking or apply diluted oil topically for pain.

name: Star Anise
preparation: Brew 1 pod in hot water as tea. Use in cooking.
safety: Avoid Japanese star anise — it is toxic. Only use Chinese star anise.

name: Nutmeg
preparation: Add small pinch to warm milk before bed for sleep.
safety: Toxic in large amounts. Use only culinary quantities.

name: Saffron
preparation: Add a few strands to warm water or milk. Take 30mg extract daily.
safety: Safe in culinary amounts. Avoid high doses in pregnancy.

name: Paprika
preparation: Use generously in cooking or as capsaicin cream topically for pain.

name: Caraway
preparation: Brew 1 tsp crushed seeds in hot water after meals.

name: Nigella
preparation: Take 1-2 tsp seeds daily or 500mg-1g oil extract daily.
safety: Generally safe. May interact with immunosuppressants.

name: Turmeric Black Pepper Blend
preparation: Mix 1 tsp turmeric with 1/4 tsp black pepper in warm water or milk daily.

name: Za'atar
preparation: Mix with olive oil and use as dip. Brew as tea.

name: Szechuan Pepper
preparation: Use small amounts in cooking. Apply topically for pain.

name: Long Pepper
preparation: Take with honey for respiratory issues. Use in Ayurvedic preparations.
safety: Avoid in high blood pressure and pregnancy.

name: Tea Tree Oil
preparation: Dilute 1-2 drops in carrier oil. Apply topically. Add 5-10 drops to bath.
safety: Never ingest. Dilute before skin use. Toxic to pets.

name: Eucalyptus Oil
preparation: Add 5 drops to steam inhaler or diffuser. Dilute and apply to chest.
safety: Do not apply near infants' faces. Toxic if ingested.

name: Frankincense Oil
preparation: Diffuse or dilute 2-3 drops in carrier oil for massage or skin application.
safety: Generally safe diluted. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Peppermint Oil
preparation: Apply diluted to temples for headache. Inhale for nausea or focus.
safety: Do not apply near infants. Keep away from eyes.

name: Lavender Oil
preparation: Add 5-10 drops to diffuser. Apply diluted to pulse points. Add to bath.
safety: Generally safe diluted. Avoid undiluted on sensitive skin.

name: Lemon Oil
preparation: Diffuse for mood lift. Add 1-2 drops to water (food-grade only). Apply diluted.
safety: Photosensitive — avoid sun exposure after topical application.

name: Rosemary Oil
preparation: Massage diluted onto scalp for hair. Diffuse for memory. Apply for muscle pain.

name: Clary Sage Oil
preparation: Dilute and apply to abdomen for menstrual pain. Diffuse for mood.
safety: Avoid in estrogen-sensitive conditions and pregnancy.

name: Bergamot Oil
preparation: Diffuse for mood. Apply diluted to skin (non-photosensitive form only).
safety: Highly photosensitive. Use bergapten-free oil topically.

name: Cedarwood Oil
preparation: Diffuse at night for sleep. Apply diluted to scalp for hair.
safety: Generally safe diluted. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Ylang Ylang Oil
preparation: Diffuse for mood and stress. Dilute and apply to pulse points.
safety: Use sparingly — strong scent may cause headache.

name: Geranium Oil
preparation: Dilute and apply to skin or abdomen. Diffuse for mood balance.
safety: Generally safe diluted. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Ginger Oil
preparation: Inhale for nausea. Dilute and apply for muscle pain and circulation.
safety: May irritate sensitive skin. Test before use.

name: Oregano Oil
preparation: Take 1-3 drops food-grade oil diluted in water. Apply diluted topically.
safety: Very potent. Always dilute. Can burn skin undiluted. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Frankincense Serrata Oil
preparation: Diffuse for spiritual practices. Dilute and apply to joints or skin.

name: Myrrh Oil
preparation: Dilute and apply to wounds or skin issues. Diffuse for relaxation.

name: Sandalwood Oil
preparation: Diffuse or dilute and apply to pulse points and skin.

name: Neroli Oil
preparation: Diffuse or add a few drops to bath or carrier oil for skin.

name: Helichrysum Oil
preparation: Dilute 2-3 drops in carrier oil. Apply to bruises, scars, or inflamed skin.

name: Vetiver Oil
preparation: Diffuse at night or apply diluted to feet and wrists before bed.

name: Green Tea
preparation: Steep 1 tsp leaves in 80°C water for 2-3 min. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
safety: Contains caffeine. Avoid in insomnia and excessive anxiety.

name: Black Tea
preparation: Steep 1 tsp in boiling water 3-5 min. Drink 1-3 cups daily.
safety: Contains caffeine. Avoid in anxiety and insomnia.

name: Oolong Tea
preparation: Steep at 85°C for 1-3 min. Drink 2-4 cups daily.

name: White Tea
preparation: Steep at 75°C for 2-3 min. Drink 2-4 cups daily.

name: Rooibos Tea
preparation: Steep 1 tsp in boiling water 5-10 min. Drink 3-6 cups daily.

name: Hibiscus Tea
preparation: Steep dried flowers in hot water 5 min. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
safety: May interact with blood pressure medications and acetaminophen.

name: Ginger Lemon Tea
preparation: Brew fresh ginger slices with lemon juice in hot water. Add honey. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
safety: Avoid in large amounts during pregnancy.

name: Peppermint Tea
preparation: Steep 1 tbsp fresh or 1 tsp dried leaves in hot water 10 min.
safety: Avoid in acid reflux and infants.

name: Licorice Root Tea
preparation: Brew 1 tsp dried root 10-15 min. Drink 1-2 cups daily, short-term.
safety: Avoid in high blood pressure. Not for long-term daily use.

name: Turmeric Golden Milk
preparation: Warm 1 cup milk, add 1 tsp turmeric, 1/4 tsp black pepper, honey. Drink nightly.

name: Lemon Verbena Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried leaves in hot water 10 min.
safety: Generally safe. Avoid in kidney disease long-term.

name: Cinnamon Tea
preparation: Brew 1 cinnamon stick in hot water 15 min.
safety: Use Ceylon cinnamon. Avoid large doses.

name: Nettle Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried nettle in hot water 10 min. Drink 3 cups daily.

name: Elderflower Tea
preparation: Brew 2 tsp dried flowers in hot water 10 min. Drink 3x daily during illness.

name: Rosehip Tea
preparation: Brew 1 tbsp dried rosehips in hot water 15 min. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

name: Dandelion Root Tea
preparation: Roast and brew 1 tbsp root in hot water 10 min. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

name: Reishi Mushroom Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried reishi powder in hot water 15 min. Drink 1-2 cups daily.
safety: May interact with blood thinners. Avoid in autoimmune conditions.

name: Tulsi Ginger Tea
preparation: Brew 5 tulsi leaves with fresh ginger slices. Add honey. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

name: Spearmint Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp in hot water 5-10 min. Drink 2 cups daily.
safety: May lower testosterone — avoid in men needing testosterone.

name: Lemongrass Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 stalks or 1 tsp dried in hot water 5-10 min.

name: Magnesium Glycinate
preparation: Take 200-400mg before bed.
safety: May cause loose stools in high doses.

name: Vitamin D3
preparation: Take 1000-5000 IU daily with fatty meal.
safety: Take with K2. Toxicity possible in very high doses.

name: Omega-3 Fish Oil
preparation: Take 1-3g EPA+DHA daily with meals.

name: Probiotics
preparation: Take 10-50 billion CFU daily on empty stomach.
safety: Generally safe. May cause initial bloating.

name: CoQ10
preparation: Take 100-200mg daily with fatty meal.

name: Zinc
preparation: Take 15-30mg daily with food.
safety: Avoid high doses long-term — can deplete copper.

name: Vitamin C
preparation: Take 500-2000mg daily in divided doses.
safety: High doses may cause GI upset.

name: Iron Bisglycinate
preparation: Take 25-50mg daily on empty stomach or with vitamin C.
safety: May cause constipation. Do not exceed recommended dose.

name: Maca Root
preparation: Take 1.5-3g powder daily in food or smoothies.

name: Spirulina
preparation: Take 1-3g daily. Start with low dose.
safety: Avoid in phenylketonuria. Check for heavy metal contamination.

name: Chlorella
preparation: Take 3-5g daily with water.
safety: May cause green stools. Start low.

name: Lion's Mane Mushroom
preparation: Take 500mg-3g daily.

name: Cordyceps
preparation: Take 1-3g daily.
rating: Generally safe. Avoid in autoimmune conditions.

name: Chaga Mushroom
preparation: Brew as tea or take 400mg extract 2x daily.
safety: Avoid with blood thinners and insulin.

name: Turkey Tail Mushroom
preparation: Take 1-3g daily.
rating: Generally safe. May cause mild GI upset.

name: Collagen Peptides
preparation: Take 10-20g daily mixed in water or food.

name: Hyaluronic Acid
preparation: Take 120-240mg daily.

name: NAC
preparation: Take 600-1800mg daily.

name: Alpha Lipoic Acid
preparation: Take 300-600mg daily.
safety: May lower blood sugar. Take with food.

name: Berberine
preparation: Take 500mg 3x daily with meals.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy. May interact with medications metabolized by CYP3A4.

name: Neem
preparation: Brew leaves as tea or apply paste to skin. Use neem oil diluted for hair and skin.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy and in children.

name: Moringa
preparation: Add 1-2 tsp powder to food or smoothies. Brew leaves as tea.

name: Bitter Leaf
preparation: Squeeze fresh leaves in water, drink 1 cup 2x daily.
safety: Avoid excessive amounts. Consult provider for malaria treatment.

name: Shea Butter
preparation: Apply unrefined shea butter directly to skin or hair.

name: Gotu Kola
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract daily or brew fresh leaves as tea.
safety: Avoid with sedatives and hepatotoxic drugs.

name: Triphala
preparation: Take 500mg-1g powder with warm water before bed.

name: Amla
preparation: Take 1-2 tsp dried powder or 500mg extract daily.

name: Shatavari
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract daily.

name: Brahmi
preparation: Take 300-450mg standardized extract daily.
safety: May interact with thyroid and Alzheimer's medications.

name: Guggul
preparation: Take 25mg guggulsterone extract 3x daily.
safety: Avoid in liver disease and pregnancy.

name: Mucuna Pruriens
preparation: Take 15-30g seed powder daily under supervision.
safety: Consult doctor if on Parkinson's medications.

name: Guduchi
preparation: Take 300mg-500mg extract 2x daily.

name: He Shou Wu
preparation: Take 9-15g dried root as decoction.
safety: Processed form only. Raw root hepatotoxic. Consult TCM practitioner.

name: Bai Zhu
preparation: Take 6-12g as decoction or in patent formula.

name: Jing Jie
preparation: Brew 6-10g as decoction.

name: Smilax
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried root 15-20 min. Drink 2-3 cups daily.

name: Graviola
preparation: Brew leaves as tea. Take 1-2 cups daily.
safety: Avoid in Parkinson's disease. Do not use excessively.

name: Dragon's Blood
preparation: Apply sap directly to wounds. Take drops in water for GI issues.
safety: Use under guidance. Strong antiviral properties.

name: Baobab Fruit
preparation: Mix 1-2 tsp powder in water or food daily.

name: Rauvolfia
preparation: Use only under medical supervision. Standardized extracts only.
safety: Strong medication interactions. Physician guidance required.

name: Ginseng Root
preparation: Take 200-400mg extract daily or brew 1-3g root as tea.
safety: Avoid in anxiety, high blood pressure, pregnancy.

name: Siberian Ginseng
preparation: Take 300-600mg extract daily.

name: American Ginseng
preparation: Take 100-200mg extract daily.
safety: May interact with blood thinners and diabetes medications.

name: Goldenseal Root
preparation: Take 250-500mg extract 3x daily short-term.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy. Not for long-term use.

name: Kava Root
preparation: Take 70-250mg kavalactones as standardized extract.
safety: Avoid with alcohol and hepatotoxic substances. Limit duration.

name: Maca Root Powder
preparation: Take 1-3g powder daily in food or smoothies.

name: Yam Root
preparation: Take 2-4g dried root daily or 40mg extract.
safety: Do not confuse with sweet potato. Avoid in hormone-sensitive cancers.

name: Chaste Tree Berries
preparation: Take 20-40mg extract daily, best in morning.
safety: Avoid with hormone medications and dopamine drugs.

name: Pau d'Arco Bark
preparation: Brew 1 tbsp bark in 4 cups water 20 min. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy and clotting disorders.

name: Slippery Elm Bark
preparation: Mix 1-2 tsp powder in warm water. Drink before meals.

name: White Willow Bark
preparation: Take 120-240mg salicin standardized extract daily.
safety: Avoid if allergic to aspirin. May interact with blood thinners.

name: Cinnamon Bark
preparation: Brew as tea or take 1-6g powder daily.
safety: Use Ceylon variety. Avoid in liver disease.

name: Quinine Bark
preparation: Used medicinally as quinine under medical supervision only.
safety: Medical supervision required. Can cause cardiac effects.

name: Black Cohosh Root
preparation: Take 20-40mg extract twice daily.
safety: Avoid in liver disease. Limit use to 6 months.

name: Marshmallow Root
preparation: Cold-infuse 2 tbsp dried root in 2 cups water overnight.

name: Bacopa
preparation: Take 300mg extract daily with fat.

name: Horsetail
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried herb as tea.
safety: Avoid long-term. Avoid in kidney disease.

name: Agrimony
preparation: Brew 2 tsp as tea 3x daily.

name: Bilberry
preparation: Take 160mg standardized extract 2x daily.

name: Bugleweed
preparation: Take tincture or 1-2g herb as tea.

name: California Poppy
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp as tea before bed.
safety: Non-addictive unlike opium poppy. Generally safe.

name: Catnip
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried herb as tea.

name: Coltsfoot
preparation: Brew as tea — short-term use only.

name: Comfrey
preparation: Apply topically as cream or poultice. Do not ingest.
rating: External use only. Contains PAs — not for internal use.

name: Cramp Bark
preparation: Brew 2 tsp bark as tea or take tincture.

name: Elecampane
preparation: Brew 1 tsp root as decoction. Take 3x daily.
safety: May cause allergic reaction in sensitive individuals.

name: Evening Primrose
preparation: Take 500mg-3g GLA-standardized oil daily.

name: Fumitory
preparation: Brew 1 tsp as tea 2x daily.

name: Goat's Rue
preparation: Take 1-2g dried herb as tea.
safety: May interact with insulin. Monitor blood sugar.

name: Ground Ivy
preparation: Brew 2 tsp as tea 3x daily.

name: Hops
preparation: Take 200-300mg extract or brew as tea before bed.
safety: Avoid in depression. May interact with sedatives.

name: Hyssop
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp as tea 3x daily.

name: Ispaghula
preparation: Take 5-10g fiber with plenty of water daily.
safety: Take with plenty of water. Avoid in bowel obstruction.

name: Jamaican Dogwood
preparation: Take tincture under supervision.

name: Kudzu
preparation: Take 1-2g root extract daily.

name: Lady's Mantle
preparation: Brew 2 tsp as tea 3x daily.

name: Lobelia
preparation: Use only in low doses under supervision.
safety: Toxic in high doses. Medical supervision required.

name: Milk Oat
preparation: Take 3-5ml tincture or brew oat straw tea.

name: Mugwort
preparation: Brew 1 tsp as tea 2x daily.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy. May trigger seizures in epilepsy.

name: Mullein
preparation: Brew 2 tsp dried leaves as tea 3x daily. Oil for earache.

name: New Zealand Green Lipped Mussel
preparation: Take 400-1200mg extract daily.

name: Prickly Ash Bark
preparation: Take 1-3ml tincture or brew 1 tsp bark as tea.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy and peptic ulcers.

name: Puncture Vine
preparation: Take 250-750mg extract daily.

name: Red Raspberry Leaf
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried leaf as tea 2-3x daily.
safety: Use in late pregnancy only under midwife supervision.

name: Saw Palmetto
preparation: Take 160mg standardized extract 2x daily.

name: Senna
preparation: Take 17-34mg sennoside at bedtime.
safety: Short-term use only. Avoid in bowel obstruction.

name: Sheep Sorrel
preparation: Brew as tea or use in Essiac blend.
safety: Avoid in kidney stones. Contains oxalic acid.

name: Solomon's Seal
preparation: Take 2-4ml tincture 3x daily or brew root as tea.

name: Spilanthes
preparation: Chew fresh herb or take 1-2ml tincture.
safety: May cause tingling sensation. Generally safe.

name: Sweet Violet
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp as tea 3x daily.

name: Tansy
preparation: Use only under supervision — toxic in large doses.
safety: Toxic. Do not use in pregnancy. Medical supervision required.

name: Turmeric Paste
preparation: Mix 1 tsp turmeric with coconut oil, apply to skin.

name: Vervain
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp as tea 3x daily.

name: Wild Yam
preparation: Take 2-4g dried root or 40mg extract daily.

name: Wood Sorrel
preparation: Eat fresh leaves sparingly. Brew as tea.
safety: Avoid large amounts — contains oxalic acid.

name: Soapwort
preparation: Use as external wash for skin conditions.

name: Kava Kava Tea
preparation: Brew root powder in water, strain well. Drink 1 cup.
safety: Avoid with alcohol. Monitor liver health.

name: Passionflower Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried herb 10 min before bed.

name: Ashwagandha Latte
preparation: Mix 1 tsp powder in warm milk with honey. Drink nightly.

name: Moringa Tea
preparation: Brew 1 tsp dried leaf powder in hot water 5 min.

name: Chaga Tea
preparation: Simmer chunks in water 30+ min or brew powder.

name: Lion's Mane Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried mushroom powder in hot water.

name: Cordyceps Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp powder in hot water or add to coffee.

name: Burdock Root Tea
preparation: Decoct 2 tsp root in 2 cups water 15 min. Drink 2 cups daily.

name: Sarsaparilla Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried root 15 min.

name: Cacao Tea
preparation: Brew 1 tbsp raw cacao in hot water. Add cinnamon.

name: Maca Latte
preparation: Blend 1 tsp maca powder in warm milk with honey.

name: Reishi Latte
preparation: Mix 1 tsp extract powder in warm oat milk. Add honey.

name: Fennel Anise Tea
preparation: Brew 1 tsp crushed fennel + anise seeds in hot water.

name: Holy Basil Lemon Tea
preparation: Brew 5 leaves with lemon juice and honey.

name: Triphala Tea
preparation: Dissolve 1/2 tsp powder in warm water. Drink before bed.

name: Brahmi Tea
preparation: Brew 1 tsp dried herb 10 min. Drink 1-2 cups daily.

name: Bitter Melon Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried herb or slices in hot water.
safety: May cause low blood sugar. Monitor levels.

name: Gymnema Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried leaf in hot water 10 min.

name: Stevia Leaf Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 fresh or dried leaves in hot water.
safety: Generally safe. Avoid in pregnancy at medicinal doses.

name: Guava Leaf Tea
preparation: Brew 4-5 fresh or 1-2 tsp dried leaves in hot water.

name: Soursop Leaf Tea
preparation: Brew 3-4 dried leaves in hot water 10 min.
safety: Do not use excessively. Avoid in Parkinson's.

name: Oregano Tea
preparation: Brew 1 tsp dried oregano in hot water 10 min.

name: Bay Leaf Tea
preparation: Brew 2-3 dried leaves in hot water 10 min.

name: Celery Seed Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp crushed seeds in hot water 10 min.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy. May interact with diuretics.

name: Coleus Tea
preparation: Take standardized extract 50mg 2-3x daily.
safety: Avoid with blood pressure and blood thinning medications.

name: Andrographis Tea
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp dried herb in hot water 10 min.
safety: Avoid in autoimmune conditions and pregnancy.

name: Astragalus Tea
preparation: Simmer 3-6g dried root 30 min. Drink 1-2 cups daily.

name: Codonopsis Tea
preparation: Simmer 6-15g root in water 30 min.

name: Licorice Peppermint Tea
preparation: Brew 1 tsp each in hot water 10 min.
safety: Short-term use. Avoid in high blood pressure.

name: Chrysanthemum Tea
preparation: Brew 4-5 dried flowers in hot water 3-5 min.
safety: Generally safe. May cause allergy in chrysanthemum-sensitive.

name: Vitamin B Complex
preparation: Take 1 B-complex capsule daily with food.

name: Selenium
preparation: Take 55-200mcg daily.
safety: Do not exceed 400mcg daily — toxicity risk.

name: Iodine
preparation: Take 150mcg daily as part of multivitamin.
safety: Do not supplement without testing. Iodine excess is harmful.

name: Chromium Picolinate
preparation: Take 200-1000mcg daily with meals.

name: Potassium
preparation: Take 99-400mg daily or increase dietary potassium.
safety: Avoid in kidney disease. Do not exceed recommended dose.

name: Copper
preparation: Take 1-2mg daily.
safety: Balance with zinc. Excess copper is toxic.

name: Manganese
preparation: Take 1.8-2.3mg daily.

name: GABA
preparation: Take 100-750mg daily.

name: 5-HTP
preparation: Take 50-300mg daily before bed.

name: L-Theanine
preparation: Take 100-400mg daily. Often combined with caffeine.

name: Melatonin
preparation: Take 0.5-5mg 30-60 min before bed.
safety: Start with lowest dose. Avoid long-term dependency.

name: L-Glutamine
preparation: Take 5-10g daily on empty stomach.

name: Glycine
preparation: Take 3-5g before bed or with meals.

name: Resveratrol
preparation: Take 150-500mg daily with meals.

name: Quercetin
preparation: Take 500-1000mg daily with bromelain for absorption.

name: Bromelain
preparation: Take 500mg 2-3x daily away from food for anti-inflammatory effect.

name: Papain
preparation: Take 50-100mg with meals or apply to skin wounds.
safety: Generally safe. May cause allergy in latex-sensitive.

name: Black Seed Oil
preparation: Take 1-2 tsp daily or 500mg capsule 2x daily.
safety: Generally safe. May lower blood pressure.

name: Sea Buckthorn
preparation: Take 5-10ml juice or 500mg capsule daily.

name: Amla Extract
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract daily.

name: Shilajit
preparation: Take 300mg purified extract daily.
safety: Use only purified form. Raw shilajit may contain heavy metals.

name: Trikatu
preparation: Take 500mg powder with honey before meals.
safety: Avoid in ulcers, pregnancy, and inflammatory bowel.

name: Chyawanprash
preparation: Take 1-2 tsp daily with warm milk.
safety: Generally safe. Contains sugar — diabetics take carefully.

name: Kumkumadi Oil
preparation: Apply a few drops to face at night, massage gently.

name: Panchakarma Blend
preparation: Under Ayurvedic practitioner guidance only.
safety: Requires supervision of trained Ayurvedic physician.

name: Dashamoola
preparation: Take 1-3g powder as decoction or under Ayurvedic guidance.

name: Shatadhautaghrita
preparation: Apply liberally to affected skin areas.

name: Arjuna
preparation: Take 500mg bark extract 2-3x daily.
safety: Avoid with heart medications without supervision.

name: Karela
preparation: Juice 1/2 bitter melon daily or take 500mg extract.
safety: May cause low blood sugar. Monitor.

name: Methi
preparation: Soak 1 tsp seeds overnight, drink water and eat seeds.

name: Pomegranate Peel
preparation: Brew dried peel as tea or use as gargle.

name: Black Plum Seed
preparation: Take seed powder 3-6g daily with water.

name: Aloe Vera
preparation: Apply gel to skin. Take 1-2 tbsp inner gel in water for digestion.
safety: Avoid latex layer internally. Not for long-term internal use.

name: Papaya Leaf
preparation: Juice fresh leaves or brew dried leaf as tea.
safety: May interact with blood thinners. Use under supervision for dengue.

name: Coconut Oil
preparation: Apply to skin/hair topically. Take 1-2 tbsp daily for internal use.
safety: Generally safe. High in saturated fat.

name: Activated Charcoal
preparation: Take 500mg-1g capsule for gas. Detox protocol under supervision.
safety: Can bind medications. Space from medications by 2 hours.

name: Apple Cider Vinegar
preparation: Dilute 1-2 tbsp in water before meals.
safety: May damage tooth enamel. Always dilute.

name: Honey Raw
preparation: Take 1 tbsp directly or dissolve in warm water. Apply to wounds.
safety: Do not give to infants under 1 year.

name: Propolis
preparation: Take 500mg extract daily or use tincture for oral health.
safety: May cause allergy in bee-sensitive individuals.

name: Royal Jelly
preparation: Take 1-2g fresh daily or 150mg dried extract.
safety: May cause severe allergy in bee-sensitive individuals.

name: Manuka Honey
preparation: Apply directly to wounds. Take 1-2 tsp for throat and gut.
safety: Generally safe. Not for infants under 1.

name: Noni Juice
preparation: Take 30-90ml daily on empty stomach.
safety: Avoid in liver disease and kidney disease.

name: Mangosteen
preparation: Take 500mg extract or drink juice daily.

name: Acerola Cherry
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract or eat 1-2 fresh fruits daily.

name: Camu Camu
preparation: Take 1-5g dried powder daily.

name: Lucuma
preparation: Take 1-2 tbsp powder in food daily.

name: Baobab
preparation: Mix 1-2 tsp in water or smoothie daily.

name: Bee Pollen
preparation: Take 1/4-1 tsp daily. Start small to check for allergy.
safety: Avoid if bee-allergic. Start with small amounts.

name: Chlorophyll
preparation: Add 1-2 tsp liquid chlorophyll to water daily.
safety: May cause green stools. Generally safe.

name: Fulvic Acid
preparation: Take 10-20 drops in water daily.
safety: Use clean source. Avoid contaminated products.

name: Shilajit Resin
preparation: Dissolve rice-grain sized amount in warm water daily.

name: Cistanche
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract daily.

name: Eucommia
preparation: Take 10-15g bark decoction or 500mg extract daily.

name: Rehmannia
preparation: Take 10-30g cooked root as decoction.

name: Ligustrum
preparation: Take 5-15g dried berry as decoction.

name: Fo-Ti
preparation: Use prepared (shou wu) form only. Take 9-15g as decoction.
safety: Raw form hepatotoxic. Use processed form only.

name: Jujube
preparation: Take 10-30g dried fruit as decoction or eat fresh fruit.

name: Longan
preparation: Take 10-15g dried fruit as tea.

name: Wolfberry Goji
preparation: Eat 20-30g dried berries daily or brew as tea.

name: Dang Shen
preparation: Simmer 10-30g root 30 min. Drink as tea.

name: Bai Shao
preparation: Take 6-15g as decoction.
safety: Avoid in cold and damp conditions.

name: Yi Yi Ren
preparation: Cook 30-60g with rice or simmer as decoction.

name: Huang Qi
preparation: Simmer 15-30g root 30 min. Drink as tea daily.

name: Dang Gui
preparation: Take 3-15g as decoction.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy and bleeding disorders.

name: Long Dan Cao
preparation: Take 3-9g as decoction. Short-term use.
safety: Cold and bitter. Avoid in deficiency.

name: Suan Zao Ren
preparation: Take 9-18g roasted seeds as decoction or powder.

name: Wu Wei Zi
preparation: Take 1.5-6g dried berry as tea.

name: Elderberry Syrup
preparation: Take 1 tbsp 4x daily during illness. 1x daily prevention.

name: Osha Root
preparation: Take 1-3ml tincture 3-5x daily during illness.

name: Usnea
preparation: Take 1-3ml tincture 3x daily.

name: Lomatium
preparation: Take 1-2ml tincture 3-4x daily.
safety: May cause skin rash initially (detox reaction).

name: Spilanthes Herb
preparation: Chew fresh herb or take 1-2ml tincture.

name: Wild Oregano
preparation: Take 1-3 drops food-grade in water. Diffuse for respiratory.

name: Cypress Oil
preparation: Dilute 2-3 drops in carrier oil. Apply or diffuse.

name: Juniper Berry Oil
preparation: Dilute and apply topically or add to bath.
safety: Avoid in kidney disease and pregnancy.

name: Thyme Oil
preparation: Dilute well before topical use. Diffuse for respiratory.

name: Clary Sage Oil
preparation: Dilute and apply to abdomen or diffuse.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy and estrogen cancers.

name: Litsea Oil
preparation: Diffuse for mood. Dilute and apply to skin.

name: Immortelle Oil
preparation: Dilute 2-3% in carrier oil. Apply to problem areas.

name: Petitgrain Oil
preparation: Diffuse or dilute in carrier oil for skin and massage.

name: Carrot Seed Oil
preparation: Dilute 1-2% in carrier oil for skin. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Blue Tansy Oil
preparation: Dilute well and apply topically. Diffuse for allergies.
safety: Not same as common tansy. Generally safe diluted.

name: Spikenard Oil
preparation: Diffuse for meditation and sleep. Dilute for skin.

name: Black Spruce Oil
preparation: Diffuse or apply diluted to adrenal area (lower back).

name: Sea Kelp
preparation: Take 150mcg iodine from kelp or sprinkle on food.
safety: Avoid in thyroid disease without testing.

name: Spirulina Capsules
preparation: Take 1-3g capsules daily with water.
safety: Check heavy metals. Avoid contaminated sources.

name: Chlorella Tablets
preparation: Take 2-5g daily. Start low to avoid detox reactions.

name: Fibre Complex
preparation: Take 5-10g with 8oz water before meals.

name: Digestive Enzymes
preparation: Take 1-2 capsules with each main meal.

name: Bone Broth Collagen
preparation: Take 10-20g daily in warm water or food.

name: Matcha Powder
preparation: Whisk 1-2 tsp in hot water (80°C). Drink 1-2 cups daily.

name: Cacao Ceremony Blend
preparation: Dissolve 20-40g raw cacao paste in hot water. Add spices.

name: Periwinkle
preparation: Use standardized vinpocetine extract under supervision.
safety: Avoid in pregnancy. May interact with blood thinners.

name: Bacopa Capsule
preparation: Take 300mg standardized extract daily with fat.

name: Ginkgo Biloba
preparation: Take 120-240mg standardized extract daily.
safety: May interact with blood thinners and seizure medications.

name: Huperzia
preparation: Take 50-200mcg huperzine A daily.
safety: Cycle use — take breaks. Avoid with anticholinergics.

name: Phosphatidylserine
preparation: Take 100-300mg daily with meals.

name: Acetyl-L-Carnitine
preparation: Take 500mg-2g daily.

name: Curcumin Complex
preparation: Take 500mg-1g BCM-95 or phytosome curcumin daily.
safety: Generally safe. Check form for absorption.

name: Quercetin Bromelain
preparation: Take 500mg with 100mg bromelain 2x daily.

name: Oregano Capsules
preparation: Take 200mg 3x daily. Use enteric coated for gut infections.
safety: Do not use long-term. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Goldenseal Capsule
preparation: Take 250-500mg extract 3x daily short-term.
safety: Not for long-term use. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Andrographis Capsule
preparation: Take 400mg 3x daily during illness.
safety: Short-term use. Avoid autoimmune and pregnancy.

name: Garlic Extract
preparation: Take 600-1200mg aged garlic extract daily.

name: Olive Leaf Extract
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract daily.

name: Grape Seed Extract
preparation: Take 100-300mg OPC extract daily.

name: Green Tea Extract
preparation: Take 400-500mg EGCG standardized extract daily.
safety: Contains caffeine. May stress liver in high doses.

name: Bilberry Extract
preparation: Take 160mg standardized extract 2x daily.

name: Hawthorn Extract
preparation: Take 160-900mg extract daily.

name: Rosehip Seed Oil
preparation: Apply 2-5 drops directly to face nightly.
safety: Generally safe. May clog pores in acne-prone.

name: Argan Oil
preparation: Apply few drops to face, hair, or nails daily.

name: Jojoba Oil
preparation: Apply to skin or scalp as needed.

name: Castor Oil
preparation: Take 1-2 tsp internally for constipation. Apply to scalp for hair.

name: Neem Oil
preparation: Dilute 1:10 in carrier oil. Apply to skin or scalp.
safety: Strong smell. Dilute well. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Brahmi Oil
preparation: Massage warm oil into scalp 30 min before washing.

name: Bhringraj Oil
preparation: Warm and apply to scalp. Leave overnight or 1 hour.

name: Amla Oil
preparation: Apply to scalp, massage, leave 1-2 hours before washing.

name: Triphala Oil
preparation: Massage into scalp 2-3 times per week.

name: Kalonji Oil
preparation: Take 1 tsp daily or apply topically to skin/hair.

name: Pumpkin Seed Oil
preparation: Take 1-2 tsp daily or 1000mg capsule.

name: Evening Primrose Oil
preparation: Take 500mg-3g daily.

name: Borage Oil
preparation: Take 500mg-1g GLA daily.

name: Flaxseed Oil
preparation: Take 1-2 tbsp daily. Must refrigerate.

name: Sesame Oil
preparation: Use for oil pulling 20 min daily. Apply to skin.

name: Tamanu Oil
preparation: Apply directly to scars, wounds, or skin problems.

name: Marula Oil
preparation: Apply few drops to face or hair as needed.

name: Emu Oil
preparation: Apply topically to painful joints or skin areas.

name: Magnesium Oil
preparation: Spray 5-10 pumps on skin, massage in. May sting initially.

name: Epsom Salt Bath
preparation: Dissolve 2 cups in warm bath. Soak 20 minutes.
safety: Generally safe. Avoid in kidney disease.

name: Hydrogen Peroxide
preparation: Dilute 3% for wound cleaning and gargling.
safety: Do not ingest. Use only 3% medical grade.

name: Colloidal Silver
preparation: Apply topically or use as nasal spray. 10ppm max.
rating: Limited evidence. May interact with antibiotics. Avoid long-term internal use.

name: Bentonite Clay
preparation: Apply as face mask. Take 1 tsp in water away from medications.

name: Diatomaceous Earth
preparation: Take 1 tsp food-grade in water daily. Start small.
safety: Food grade only. Avoid inhaling powder.

name: Zeolite
preparation: Take 1-3g powder daily in water.

name: DMSO
preparation: Apply 70% solution topically to painful areas.
safety: Medical grade only. Avoid contamination. Consult physician.

name: MSM
preparation: Take 1-3g daily with meals.
safety: Generally safe. May cause initial detox.

name: Glucosamine
preparation: Take 1500mg daily with food.
safety: May raise blood sugar. Avoid in shellfish allergy.

name: Chondroitin
preparation: Take 800-1200mg daily with food.

name: Hyaluronic Acid Serum
preparation: Apply to face twice daily or take 120mg capsule daily.

name: Retinol
preparation: Take 2500-10000 IU daily. Apply topical retinol at night.
safety: Teratogenic in high doses. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Niacinamide
preparation: Take 500mg-3g daily or apply 5% serum to skin.
safety: Flushing at high doses. Use non-flush form.

name: Pantothenic Acid
preparation: Take 500mg-10g daily.

name: Biotin
preparation: Take 2.5-10mg daily.
safety: Generally safe. May affect lab tests.

name: Folate
preparation: Take 400-800mcg daily. Use methylfolate for MTHFR.

name: Vitamin K2
preparation: Take 90-200mcg daily with vitamin D3.
safety: Generally safe. Take with D3 and calcium.

name: Vitamin E
preparation: Take 200-400 IU daily with meals.

name: Astaxanthin
preparation: Take 4-12mg daily with a fatty meal.

name: Lutein
preparation: Take 10-20mg daily with fatty meal.

name: Lycopene
preparation: Take 15-45mg daily. Best absorbed from cooked tomatoes.

name: Beta Glucan
preparation: Take 250mg-3g daily.

name: Lactoferrin
preparation: Take 100-300mg daily.

name: Colostrum
preparation: Take 500mg-5g daily on empty stomach.
safety: Generally safe. Avoid in milk allergy.

name: Digestive Bitters
preparation: Take 1-2ml tincture before meals.

name: Artichoke Leaf
preparation: Take 320-1800mg extract daily.
safety: Avoid in gallstones and bile duct obstruction.

name: Milk Thistle Extract
preparation: Take 140mg silymarin 3x daily with meals.

name: Dandelion Extract
preparation: Take 500-2000mg extract daily.

name: Beet Root Extract
preparation: Take 500mg-6g daily or drink 200ml beet juice.
safety: May cause red/pink urine. Generally safe.

name: Tart Cherry
preparation: Take 480mg concentrate or drink 240ml juice daily.

name: Pomegranate Extract
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract daily.
safety: May interact with blood pressure medications.

name: Blueberry Extract
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract daily or eat 1 cup fresh berries.

name: Cranberry Extract
preparation: Take 400-500mg PAC standardized extract daily.

name: Elderberry Extract
preparation: Take 175-1000mg extract daily.

name: Echinacea Extract
preparation: Take 300-500mg 3x daily during illness.

name: Grape Juice Concentrate
preparation: Take 120-240ml daily.

name: Acai Berry
preparation: Take 1000mg freeze-dried powder or 100ml juice daily.

name: Maqui Berry
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract daily.

name: Noni Extract
preparation: Take 500mg extract or 30ml juice daily.
safety: Avoid in liver and kidney disease.

name: Black Currant
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract or 60ml juice daily.

name: Lingonberry
preparation: Take 1000mg extract or eat berries regularly.

name: Schisandra Extract
preparation: Take 500mg-1g extract daily.

name: Holy Basil Extract
preparation: Take 300-600mg extract daily.

name: Rhodiola Extract
preparation: Take 200-400mg standardized extract daily.

name: Maca Extract
preparation: Take 1500-3000mg extract daily.

name: Tongkat Ali
preparation: Take 200-400mg standardized extract daily.

name: Tribulus Terrestris
preparation: Take 250-750mg extract daily.

name: Fenugreek Extract
preparation: Take 500-600mg extract daily.

name: Ashwagandha Extract
preparation: Take 300-600mg KSM-66 or Sensoril extract daily.
safety: Avoid in autoimmune conditions and pregnancy.

name: Zinc Citrate
preparation: Take 15-30mg daily with food.

name: Magnesium Threonate
preparation: Take 1500-2000mg daily.

name: Lithium Orotate
preparation: Take 5-20mg elemental lithium daily.
safety: Consult doctor. Very different from prescription lithium.

name: Inositol
preparation: Take 2-4g daily.

name: NAD+ Precursor
preparation: Take 250-500mg daily.

name: Pterostilbene
preparation: Take 50-200mg daily.

name: Fisetin
preparation: Take 100-500mg daily.

name: Apigenin
preparation: Take 50-100mg daily.

name: Spermidine
preparation: Take 1-5mg daily. Eat wheat germ, fermented foods.

name: Urolithin A
preparation: Take 500-1000mg daily.

name: PQQ
preparation: Take 10-40mg daily.

name: Hydrogen Water
preparation: Drink H2-infused water or use H2 tablets in water.

name: Sauerkraut
preparation: Eat 1-2 tbsp daily with meals.
safety: May cause initial gas. Generally safe.

name: Kimchi
preparation: Eat 1-2 tbsp daily with meals.

name: Kefir
preparation: Drink 200-400ml daily.
safety: Generally safe. Lower lactose than milk.

name: Kombucha
preparation: Drink 120-240ml daily.
safety: Avoid in immunocompromised. Small alcohol content.

name: Miso
preparation: Use 1-2 tsp in soups daily. Do not boil to preserve probiotics.

name: Natto
preparation: Eat 50-100g daily. Acquired taste.
rating: Generally safe. Avoid with blood thinners.

name: Tempeh
preparation: Eat 100-200g daily.

name: Coconut Water
preparation: Drink 240-480ml daily or after exercise.
safety: Generally safe. Avoid in kidney disease.

name: Aloe Vera Juice
preparation: Drink 30-60ml inner fillet juice daily.
safety: Use decolorized/purified juice. Avoid latex form.

name: Wheatgrass
preparation: Take 1-2 tsp powder or 30ml juice daily.

name: Barley Grass
preparation: Take 1-3 tsp powder daily in water.

name: Oat Straw
preparation: Brew 3-5g as tea or take as tincture.

name: Wood Betony
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp as tea 3x daily.

name: Self-Heal
preparation: Apply to wounds or brew as tea for internal use.

name: Healing Mushroom Blend
preparation: Take 1-3g blend daily.

name: Watercress
preparation: Add to salads or juice. Brew as tea.

name: Parsley
preparation: Brew 2 tsp as tea or juice fresh leaves.

name: Cilantro
preparation: Add fresh to food. Juice or brew as tea.

name: Dill
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp seeds in hot water.

name: Tarragon
preparation: Use in cooking or brew as tea.

name: Lemon Thyme
preparation: Brew as tea or use in cooking.

name: Spearmint
preparation: Brew 1-2 tsp fresh leaves in hot water.

name: Apple Mint
preparation: Brew as tea or add to food.

name: Grapefruit Seed Extract
preparation: Take 100-200mg extract or 10-15 drops in water 3x daily.

name: Lemon Extract
preparation: Add 2 tbsp fresh lemon juice to water daily.

name: Lime Leaf
preparation: Brew fresh leaves as tea.

name: Orange Peel
preparation: Brew dried peel as tea or use zest in cooking.

name: Mandarin Peel
preparation: Brew dried peel as tea in TCM.

name: Lychee
preparation: Eat fresh or take extract.

name: Dragon Fruit
preparation: Eat fresh regularly or take extract.

name: Jackfruit Seed
preparation: Roast and eat seeds. Take seed powder.

name: Tamarind
preparation: Eat pulp or brew as drink.

name: Hibiscus Roselle
preparation: Brew dried calyces as tea. Drink 2-3 cups daily.
safety: May interact with blood pressure meds.

name: Zobo Drink
preparation: Brew zobo with cloves and ginger. Drink daily.

name: Tiger Nut
preparation: Eat raw, soaked, or as horchata drink.

name: Shea Leaf Tea
preparation: Brew leaves as tea for fever and pain.

name: Locust Bean
preparation: Use in soups. Take fermented version (dawadawa).

name: African Basil
preparation: Brew leaves as tea or apply to wounds.

name: Bush Pepper
preparation: Use as spice. Brew as tea.

name: Bitter Kola
preparation: Chew 1-2 seeds daily.

name: Kola Nut
preparation: Chew fresh nut or brew as tea.
safety: Contains caffeine. Avoid in heart conditions.

name: Miracle Fruit
preparation: Eat fresh berry before meals.

name: Calabash
preparation: Juice the gourd or brew as tea.
safety: Avoid bitter varieties — can be toxic.

name: Uziza Leaf
preparation: Brew as tea or use in soups.

name: Scent Leaf
preparation: Brew fresh leaves as tea. Apply to skin.

name: Mango Leaf
preparation: Brew 5-6 fresh leaves in hot water. Drink morning.

name: Papaya Seeds
preparation: Take 1-2 tsp crushed seeds with honey.

name: Turmeric Milk
preparation: Warm 1 cup milk with 1 tsp turmeric, black pepper, honey.

name: Ghee
preparation: Take 1-2 tsp daily or use in cooking.

name: Black Salt
preparation: Add pinch to water or food for digestion.

name: Tejpat
preparation: Brew 2-3 leaves in hot water. Use in cooking.

name: Vijaysar
preparation: Use wooden cup. Soak overnight, drink water morning.

name: Punarnava
preparation: Take 3-6g powder as decoction or 500mg extract.

name: Kutki
preparation: Take 200-400mg extract daily.

name: Healing Blend
preparation: Combine based on individual needs with herbalist guidance.

name: Mineral Broth
preparation: Simmer bones with herbs 12-24 hours. Drink 1-2 cups daily.

name: Nature's Tea Blend
preparation: Brew equal parts 10 min. Drink before bed.

name: Detox Tea Blend
preparation: Brew equal parts 10 min. Drink 2x daily.

name: Immune Tea Blend
preparation: Brew equal parts 15 min. Drink 3x daily during illness.

name: Wellness Tonic
preparation: Mix 1 tsp each in warm water or milk daily.

name: Digestive Herbal Blend
preparation: Take as blended capsule or brew as tea after meals.

name: Sleep Formula
preparation: Take 30-60 min before bed.

name: Pain Relief Blend
preparation: Take as blended capsule 2-3x daily.

name: Heart Health Formula
preparation: Take as blended formula daily.
safety: Avoid with heart medications without supervision.

name: Stress Formula
preparation: Take 2 capsules daily.

name: Women's Wellness
preparation: Take 2 capsules daily.

name: Men's Vitality
preparation: Take 2 capsules daily.

name: Cognitive Support
preparation: Take 2 capsules daily.

name: Gut Restore
preparation: Take on empty stomach daily.

name: Anti-Aging Formula
preparation: Take 2 capsules daily with meals.

name: Blood Sugar Support
preparation: Take 2 capsules with meals.

name: Liver Detox Formula
preparation: Take 2 capsules daily.

name: Yarrow Extract
preparation: Take 250mg extract 3x daily.

name: Nature's Multivitamin
preparation: Take 2-4 capsules daily with meals.
